channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: 'Art Isn't Easy' author offers new insights into Stephen Sondheim's life and music
item.description: Biographer Daniel Okrent discusses Sondheim's approach to writing music and lyrics, his often toxic relationship with his mother and his work with mentors and collaborators.
item.content:encoded: Biographer Daniel Okrent discusses Sondheim's approach to writing music and lyrics, his often toxic relationship with his mother and his work with mentors and collaborators.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: Team USA won the second-most medals at these Paralympics. See the standout moments
item.description: A mix of decorated veterans and rising stars won 24 medals for Team USA, 13 of them gold. The last one arrived Sunday, when the U.S. sled hockey team beat Canada to win its fifth straight gold medal.
item.content:encoded:
A mix of decorated veterans and rising stars won 24 medals for Team USA, 13 of them gold. The last one arrived Sunday, when the U.S. sled hockey team beat Canada to win its fifth straight gold medal.

(Image credit: Stefano Rellandini)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: There's room for everyone in 'Now I Surrender,' an epic American Western
item.description: Mexican novelist Álvaro Enrigue re-imagines the story of the American West — and the Apache fight for survival — in an epic that's both defiantly challenging and, at times, magical.
item.content:encoded: Mexican novelist Álvaro Enrigue re-imagines the story of the American West — and the Apache fight for survival — in an epic that's both defiantly challenging and, at times, magical.
item.dc:creator: Maureen Corrigan

item.title: 75 years ago, a viral TV moment ignited America's obsession with the Mafia
item.description: Crooked contracts, bribery, shady characters. In 1951, millions tuned in to watch the Kefauver organized crime hearings, showing the power of television.
item.content:encoded:
Crooked contracts, bribery, shady characters. In 1951, millions tuned in to watch the Kefauver organized crime hearings, showing the power of television.

(Image credit: Bettmann Archive)
item.dc:creator: Steve Drummond

item.title: 5 takeaways from an Oscars night that spread the love
item.description: It's thrilling to see the Academy recognize a weird, funny, scary performance like Amy Madigan's in Weapons. Here's what NPR critic Linda Holmes thought of the awards.
item.content:encoded:
It's thrilling to see the Academy recognize a weird, funny, scary performance like Amy Madigan's in Weapons. Here's what NPR critic Linda Holmes thought of the awards.

(Image credit: Mike Coppola)
item.dc:creator: Linda Holmes

item.title: 'One Battle After Another' takes best picture. Here's the full list of Oscar winners
item.description: Michael B. Jordan and Jessie Buckley won best actor and best actress. Paul Thomas Anderson received best director. Cassandra Kulukundis won the Academy's first ever casting award.
item.content:encoded:
Michael B. Jordan and Jessie Buckley won best actor and best actress. Paul Thomas Anderson received best director. Cassandra Kulukundis won the Academy's first ever casting award.

(Image credit: Kevin Winter)
item.dc:creator: Clare Lombardo

item.title: See the Oscars red carpet looks
item.description: See looks from best actor Michael B. Jordan, best actress Jessie Buckley and more stars on the Oscars red carpet on Sunday night.
item.content:encoded:
See looks from best actor Michael B. Jordan, best actress Jessie Buckley and more stars on the Oscars red carpet on Sunday night.

(Image credit: Lexie Moreland)
item.dc:creator: Meredith Nierman

item.title: Cloudy with a chance of meatballs: Kentucky marks the day meat fell from the sky
item.description: In rural Kentucky, people recently celebrated the meat shower of 1876. That's when mysterious pieces of meat fell from the sky onto a farm, sparking all kinds of theories.
item.content:encoded: In rural Kentucky, people recently celebrated the meat shower of 1876. That's when mysterious pieces of meat fell from the sky onto a farm, sparking all kinds of theories.
item.dc:creator: Cheri Lawson

item.title: NPR critics discuss the frontrunners for top awards at the Oscars
item.description: The Academy Awards are tonight and critics are predicting what will win Best Picture and who will win Best Actor and Actress. We hear about the nominees and our critic's picks.
item.content:encoded: The Academy Awards are tonight and critics are predicting what will win Best Picture and who will win Best Actor and Actress. We hear about the nominees and our critic's picks.
item.dc:creator: Aisha Harris

item.title: Sunday Puzzle: BAD!
item.description: Ayesha Rascoe plays the puzzle with KCUR listener Justin Marciniak and Weekend Edition Puzzlemaster Will Shortz.
item.content:encoded: Ayesha Rascoe plays the puzzle with KCUR listener Justin Marciniak and Weekend Edition Puzzlemaster Will Shortz.
item.dc:creator: Will Shortz
